intent: Define the SecurityRecipes MCP STDIO launch boundary so local MCP server configuration is reviewed as code execution before an agent host starts a subprocess. The model gives enterprise AI platform teams a deterministic allow, hold, deny, or kill decision for command, package, sandbox, environment, network, filesystem, and approval risk.

positioning.buyer: AI Platform, Product Security, Endpoint Security, IAM, GRC, and enterprise developer platform teams
positioning.enterprise_promise: SecurityRecipes can evaluate local STDIO MCP launches before execution, prove which commands are approved, and block shell, package-runner, secret, network, and privilege drift with machine-readable evidence.
positioning.why_now: MCP adoption has made local tools easy to add, but STDIO launches are subprocess execution requests. Enterprises need a launch boundary that is just as explicit as remote MCP authorization, especially as agents, skills, and local MCP servers become part of the production control plane.

standards_alignment.name: Model Context Protocol Transports
standards_alignment.coverage: Defines STDIO as client-launched subprocess communication over standard input and output, making launch configuration part of the security boundary.

standards_alignment.name: Model Context Protocol Security Best Practices
standards_alignment.coverage: Calls out local MCP server compromise, startup command abuse, sandboxing, consent, filesystem controls, network controls, and scope minimization.

standards_alignment.name: Model Context Protocol Authorization
standards_alignment.coverage: Clarifies that HTTP authorization guidance does not apply to STDIO launches, which means credentials and local process execution need separate policy.

standards_alignment.name: OpenAI Agents SDK MCP Guide
standards_alignment.coverage: Documents hosted, Streamable HTTP, and STDIO MCP server options, reinforcing that production agent stacks must govern all transport modes.

standards_alignment.name: OWASP Top 10 for Agentic Applications 2026
standards_alignment.coverage: Maps to tool misuse, identity and privilege abuse, agentic supply chain vulnerabilities, unexpected code execution, cascading failures, and rogue agents.

standards_alignment.name: OWASP Agentic Skills Top 10
standards_alignment.coverage: Covers the execution layer behind agent skills, including verified publishers, pinned versions, isolated runtime environments, network restrictions, monitoring, and installation approval.

standards_alignment.name: NIST AI Risk Management Framework
standards_alignment.coverage: Supports Govern, Map, Measure, and Manage practices for AI systems, including 2026 critical-infrastructure profile work and generative AI risk management.

standards_alignment.name: Google Secure AI Framework
standards_alignment.coverage: Aligns with secure-by-default AI systems, detection and response for AI, harmonized platform controls, adaptive mitigations, and business-context risk handling.

decisions.meaning: The launch matches a registered command, package, sandbox, environment, network, filesystem, and evidence boundary.
The launch is registered but is missing evidence, sandboxing, approval, or narrowed environment and network controls.
The launch ID or profile is unknown and cannot start without intake.
The launch depends on package-runner bootstrap, floating versions, unverified publishers, or missing digest/signature evidence.
The launch uses shell wrappers, dangerous argument chaining, broad network bootstrap, private-network reachability, or command drift.
The launch attempts to expose secrets, inherited credentials, privileged execution, or prohibited host access.

launch_profiles.title: Pinned Read-Only Python Module
launch_profiles.description: Source-controlled Python launch with exact command allowlist, reviewed dependencies, narrow environment, and read-only evidence access.

launch_profiles.title: Pinned Node Package STDIO
launch_profiles.description: Node-based STDIO server with package lock, digest evidence, verified publisher, no install-on-launch, and sandboxed filesystem/network access.

launch_profiles.title: Containerized STDIO Server
launch_profiles.description: Container launch with image digest pin, no privileged container mode, read-only mounts, explicit network policy, and no inherited host secrets.

launch_profiles.title: Package Runner Bootstrap
launch_profiles.description: Launches through npx, uvx, pipx, bunx, or another package runner. This is denied unless the package is pre-resolved, pinned by digest, verified, and moved to a safer profile.

launch_profiles.title: Shell Bootstrap
launch_profiles.description: Shell-wrapped launch that can chain commands, download payloads, or hide behavior behind quoting. This profile is denied by default.

red_team_drills.name: Replace an approved command with a shell wrapper that chains package install and network download.
Inject API keys, bearer tokens, SSH agent sockets, or cloud credentials into a local MCP server environment.
Change a pinned source-controlled launch into npx, uvx, pipx, or bunx with a floating package version.
Enable private-network or metadata endpoint reachability from a local research MCP server.
Start a high-impact local server without sandbox evidence or typed owner approval.

enterprise_adoption_packet.board_level_claim: Local MCP STDIO launches are governed as executable supply-chain events, not developer convenience settings.
default_questions_answered: Which local MCP server commands are approved to launch?
Which launches can install packages or execute shell wrappers?
Which environment keys, network hosts, and filesystem roots can the process see?
Which launch changes require owner approval or full connector intake?
Which runtime decision should an agent host enforce before spawning the process?
enterprise_adoption_packet.recommended_first_use: Run this pack in MCP client configuration review, developer endpoint hardening, local connector approval, and one-click MCP install gating.
enterprise_adoption_packet.sales_motion: Lead with open launch-boundary evidence, then sell hosted MCP client posture management, enterprise endpoint policy export, launch receipts, and continuous local-server recertification.
